result:
**Goal: Add a headless batch-rendering mode that renders high-resolution PNG frames of the simulation.**

The project already exports GIF (low-res, 4×4 blocks) and does live terminal rendering, but has no way to produce publication-quality still images or frame sequences. A `--render` CLI mode would run the simulation headlessly for N generations and output anti-aliased PNG frames at arbitrary resolution — useful for creating wallpapers, poster prints, or feeding into `ffmpeg` for HD video. This would be implemented as a pure-Python PNG encoder (keeping the zero-dependency philosophy) with configurable cell size, color palette, and grid lines.
